calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-16-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marc Seidenfeld for (executor) Albert Benjamin. Jeff Kirk for (claimant) Global Discoveries Ltd.

The Court stated that this is a 2nd amended report, that there is an issue regarding the request to pay Global Discoveries $87,000.00 and that they want more, that the court is concerned that it is too much, that there is no indication as to the hours put into the project, that most of the information on the beneficiaries was given to Global Discoveries, that it does understand that they set this in motion and that the question is what is reasonable or legal standard. Mr. Kirk stated that Global's position is they have a contract that should be honored, cited 11604, that the record is clear that absent Global's efforts no one would get anything, that it is a 33% contingency fee, that no one has objected and that one person did not sign due to creditor issues. The Court stated that $87,000.00 for two hours of work is unreasonable, that the court has not been told what effort has gone into this and that the court is looking for better information. Mr. Kirk stated that Global does not keep detailed records or track of time. The Court stated that they may want to start. Mr. Kirk stated that they estimate 100's of hours and that the information was submitted in the brief and declaration. The Court stated that it has the declaration, that it did not indicate 100's of hours spent, that often the assignee spends a lot of time but that here it was not a question of tracking down heirs as the information was handed to Global by the executor. Mr. Kirk stated that Global found everyone including the executor. The Court stated that Global says that information other than that of the widow was given to them and that the declaration of the executor says that the information was given to Global. The Court stated that if Mr. Kirk wants the opportunity to supplement it can do that otherwise it will work with what it has. Mr. Seidenfeld stated that they have no objection to Global getting 1/3 except from the part of McAdam who refused to sign. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jan-02-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marc Seidenfeld for Petitioner.

Mr. Seidenfeld stated that there are no objections and that none have been filed. The Court expressed concern about the delay in probate/probate being held up to allow time for a third party who was seeking to get commissions from the beneficiaries for its work. Mr. Seidenfeld explained that the property was foreclosed upon, that the widow moved out of State and was not notified that there was any money left, that nobody knew about the money or where it was, that the beneficiaries were contacted by Global Discoveries and that he did an extensive search for the will. The Court granted the petition for probate of lost will and letters testamentary (subject to review of the order). Bond is waived.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Nov-24-2014 Continued To Dec-22-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
